calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Sep-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RUSSELL A. ROBINSON, TEL. NO. 1-415-861-4416 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. THAI NGUYEN, TEL. NO. 1-415-671-9901 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KHANH VU HUYNH'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT is DENIED. Defendant Huynh fails to meet his burden of proof to set aside the entry of default judgment established in In re Marriage of Kieturakis (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 56, 80. Defendant Huynh's reply does not contest Plaintiff's allegation Defendant Huynh resided at 1435 5th Avenue in Oakland four days before substituted service occurred at that resident. In addition, Defendant Huynh provides no copies of lease agreements, mail received at another address, or declarations by third parties to demonstrate that he did not live at 1435 5th Avenue in Oakland in 2011. In the absence of evidence of extrinsic fraud or mistake, applications for relief from default based upon excusable neglect must be brought within six-months after an entry of default. (Marianos v. Tutunjian (1977) 70 Cal. App. 3d 61, 64; Cal. Rules of Court, rules 62, 63.) Here, Defendant Huynh seeks to set aside a default judgment more than a year after this court entered default judgment. Defendant Huynh provides no justification for missing the six month deadline. To warrant relief under section 473 a litigant's neglect must have been such as might have been the act of a reasonably prudent person under the same circumstances. The inadvertence contemplated by the statute does not mean mere inadvertence in the abstract. If it is wholly inexcusable it does not justify relief." (Hearn v. Howards (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1193, 1206.) Defendant Huyhn provides no evidence that Plaintiff's counsel told him "not to worry" about the lawsuit. Even if they had, no reasonable person would have declined to participate in litigation in which they are a named defendant because plaintiff's counsel said "not to worry." Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
